behavior_summary: Responds to a hit on a specific item type (the FIREPUZ prototype) by spawning an asynchronous helper to play effects, animate, spawn loot, and then removes the original item immediately.
The main handler (FIREPUZ::gotHit) guards on a hit-flag parameter and the item's type; it only acts when the hit-flag is non-zero and Item::getType(item) == 0x0310.

The helper (FIREPUZ::00C7) resets kernel references for the context, plays visual/sound effects, runs a short multi-frame animation with timed pauses, reads an internal quality value and uses it to choose and create several loot items, sets/adjusts qualities on spawned items, touches them to update state, and finally destroys a helper-owned item/context.
notable_control_flow: Early-guard/fast-exit in U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit: returns immediately if the hit-flag byte parameter is zero.
Type-check gate in U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit: proceeds only when Item::getType(...) == 0x0310.
Asynchronous handoff: U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit spawns U8P_FIREPUZ::00C7 with the item and pid/context, then calls Item::destroy on the original item so visible/loot work happens independently.

U8P_FIREPUZ::00C7 performs sequential steps: Kernel::resetRef, spawn effect (FREE::2A12), looped Item::setFrame calls interleaved with FREE::1C19 delays to animate, then branching logic that creates/pops multiple loot items and sets their qualities before cleanup.
Loot-creation branch choice appears to be driven by a read-and-incremented Item::getQuality value (used as a seed/accumulator) which then influences which prototype ids and how many items are popped.
open_questions: Exact meanings of numeric constants passed to FREE::2A12 and FREE::1C19 (specific effect ids, durations or flags) remain unclear.
Precise semantic meaning of the item-type constant 0x0310 (inferred as FIREPUZ) and the variants of prototype ids used for loot are not explicitly named here.
Whether the quality accumulator read from Item::getQuality represents player-influenced quality, durability, or an internal RNG/seed is inferred but not certain.
Exact semantics of Kernel::resetRef(0x0203, pid) — whether it clears a specific process state, reference count, or a visual attachment — is not fully determined.

main_function.name: U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a hit event on the FIREPUZ item: validate the hit and item type; if valid, spawn an asynchronous helper to play effects/animation and generate loot, then destroy the original item.
entry_conditions: Invoked on a hit event (Event 6 / U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit).
Only proceeds when the hit-flag/slot parameter (byte at BP+0Ah) is non-zero.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPUZ::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for hit events on the FIREPUZ prototype; performs guards then delegates to helper and destroys the source item.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPUZ::00C7
function_map.role: Asynchronous routine that resets references, plays effects, animates the (now separate) item/process, spawns multiple loot items with adjusted qualities, and performs cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Engine call used to query the prototype/type id of an item.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine call used to remove an item instance from the world.

function_map.name: Kernel::resetRef
function_map.role: Engine/kernel call used to reset or clear a reference/state for the pid/context before effects/animation.

function_map.name: FREE::2A12
function_map.role: Spawned effect/process, inferred to be visual/sound/particle effect used at helper start and on each loot spawn.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned short timer/delay or small effect used to pace animation/frame changes.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Set the sprite/frame for an item to create an animation sequence.

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Read an item's numeric quality/attribute used to seed branch/quantity logic.

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: Write an item's quality/attribute to adjust spawned loot value.

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Allocate/create a new item instance of a given prototype prior to popping it into the world.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place a created item into the world/inventory with specified prototype/flags.

function_map.name: Item::touch
function_map.role: Mark an item as updated so the engine refreshes its visible state.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn an asynchronous helper to perform animation/effects/loot creation so the main handler can remove the source item immediately.

external_calls.purpose: Check the prototype/type id of the hit item to ensure the behavior only triggers for the FIREPUZ type (compared to 0x0310).
Destroy the original FIREPUZ item after spawning the helper so visuals/loot are handled by the helper process.
Reset/clear kernel-level reference/state for the pid/context before running effects/animation (preparation/cleanup step).
Spawn visual/sound/particle effects at helper start and when spawning each loot piece to augment the destruction animation.
Spawn short timers/delays tied to the pid/context to pace frame changes and iterations (acts as a suspend/wait).
Advance or change the displayed frame of the helper's item to animate breaking/explosion sequences.
Read a numeric quality from the item into a local accumulator that influences which loot branch to execute and values assigned to spawned items.
Create new item instances (intermediate step before Item::pop) used for loot generation.
Place created loot items into the world/inventory with specific prototype ids and flags.
Adjust the quality/value on spawned loot items using the accumulator derived from Item::getQuality.
Mark spawned/modified items as updated so the engine acknowledges their altered state (e.g., changed quality or frame).
Cleanup/destroy the helper-owned item/context at the end of the sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to a hit on a specific item type (the FIREPUZ prototype) by spawning an asynchronous helper to play effects, animate, spawn loot, and then removes the original item immediately.
